item.title: Dubya does something right
item.description: https://www.rawstory.com/george-bush-fighting-donald-trump/ I never dreamed that someone could stand next to Dubya and make him look better, but I discovered trump could and did do that. I’m glad he’s publicly supporting non-trumper candidates, although really we should all vote Democrat. Just my opinion. So hat tip, for the very first time, to George W. Bush.

Trump might be bad but he is VERY pro Jew... Choose another messiah.
Non fascist, pro love dog tax
item.dc:creator: Thehopsfarmer

item.title: Walking, running, crouching, sitting, with my DIY, free-standing VR support rig
item.description: Previous post - https://imgur.com/gallery/flYOYSG For the past several years I've been working on making VR shoes. Shoes you wear while playing a VR game that allow you to walk in the game but stay in the same spot in real life. Here I am walking in Half Life Alyx, fighting some antlions. I walk show walking backwards a few times in this clip. * I'm using the joystick on the controller to move in game. I'll add feet tracking later for PCVR, maybe for the Oculus Quest too. * There are no motors in the shoes, they are passive. I've made motorized shoes in the past and will come back to that later. The passive shoes have stoppers on the front and back to stop the shoes when the user lifts his toes or heel. * This is a DIY project and is open-source (see links below). * It takes some practice to use them. I got the hang of walking in a few minutes. My wife in 20 minutes. It took a few hours to turn and walk simultaneously. As you can see I can walk backwards, but it still feels a bit awkward. * You push against the climbing harness to bring your feet back. It's not the most natural walking feeling, but for me it's close enough and a ton of fun. * Real-life audio captured with a microphone on the chair next to me. YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCTYWMq6SbZFGOOzJxhEY1HA TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@finallyfunctional Twitter: https://twitter.com/FinallyFunct Hackaday: https://hackaday.io/projects/hacker/1167645 Discord: https://discord.com/invite/w3eSp5P Github: https://github.com/finallyfunctional
item.dc:creator: finallyfunctional

item.title: some common myths debunked
item.description:
Myth: You can target your fat burn. Fact: Working out can reduce overall fat, but you can’t control what part of your body burns the most fat. Your body breaks down fat and uses it as fuel when you exercise. But your body’s not picky. It’ll burn fat from anywhere in your body, not just the part you’re working the most.
Myth: We have five senses Fact: Besides the five empirical senses, sight, sound, touch, taste and smell, there are at least four other senses that are rarely cited proprioception—sensing one's orientation in space thermoception—sense of temperature equilibrioception—sense of balance interoception—sense of one's physiological condition.
It's a myth that: Bulls become angry when they see the color red. Fact: It's not the color of the cape that angers the bull but it’s the movement of the cape. We know this based on many experiments with different colored capes being stationary and being moved and the bull favored movement over the color of the cape every time. We also know that bulls are actually red-green colorblind and would have a hard time distinguishing red from green, orange, and brown.
Myth: Marie Antoinette is quoted as having famously said "let them eat cake". Fact: There's zero evidence that she ever said it or something like it. The original claim was that she said the peasants should eat brioche. But there are examples from earlier folklore where oblivious aristocrats show their ignorance by telling the starving poor to simply eat rich luxurious cake.
It's a myth that: Penis size varies according to race. Fact: This idea is not supported by scientific evidence. According to a urologist named Aaron Spitz, many studies and websites promoting variation of penis size between races use unscientific methods of collecting information (such as self-reporting which is very unreliable), and often ignore contradictory evidence. https://www.dailydot.com/irl/penis-size-infographic-debunked/
Myth: A penny dropped from the empire state building can kill a person on the sidewalk Fact: According to the myth the penny would accelerate to the point that it could kill a person on the sidewalk below. But in reality, the penny will reach terminal velocity of about 30-100 miles per hour depending on the wind. If you dropped a penny from up there it might hurt, but it wouldn't kill anyone.
It's a myth that: Goldfish have a 3-7 second memory Fact: Goldfish can learn basic survival skills and remember them for up to three months. They can even tell time. In lab tests they could be conditioned to push a lever for food at roughly the same time each day.
Myth: Vikings wore horns on their helmets. Fact: Most vikings never did. Some of them may have had horns affixed to their gear, but they mostly had normal metal helmets. Wagner's 1876 opera The Ring of Nibelung popularized this image into our minds.
It's a myth that: You should wait 24 hours before reporting a missing person. Fact: There is no need to wait 24 hours, at least not anymore. Call 911. In fact, taking action within the first 48 hours is crucial to bringing a missing child home. The No. 1 biggest problem is that family members wait too long to report a missing persons case. You lose valuable time during those 24 hours.
Myth: The ingredients in COVID-19 vaccines are dangerous. Fact: Nearly all the ingredients in COVID-19 vaccines are also ingredients in many foods – fats, sugars, and salts. Exact vaccine ingredients vary by manufacturer. COVID-19 vaccines do NOT contain ingredients like preservatives, tissues (like aborted fetal cells), antibiotics, food proteins, medicines, latex, or metals.

item.dc:creator: hoodbobthugpants

item.title: Why Maus Matters
item.description:
This Twitter thread reflects a lot of the conversation I've heard in the Jewish community lately about why books like Maus matter and why books like The Boy in the Striped Pajamas don't quite cut it. It's worth reading, but TL:DR: Boy in the Striped Pajamas is written to make people feel comfortable about the Holocaust being a General Sad Thing with neat lessons to learn about bigotry. It's a non Jewish perspective, with non of the sense of the Jewish world that was lost. Maus is a real story, with real implications.
When my grandma was a child in 1930s Germany, other kids threw stones at her for beinga Jew when she tried to play outside.
My grandma was a survivor and that intergenerational trauma thing is real.
Another post I saw described The Boy in the Striped Pajamas of being an example of Holocaust historical fiction that depicts "Good Gentiles Doing Good Things." Even though the Righteous among the nations (including those that saved my grandmother) were a stasticial anomaly, these stories focus on them, helping their non Jewish readership feel more comfortable and less reflective.
item.dc:creator: AngryTeacher

item.title: Wholesome post to brighten your day 596
item.description:
https://imgur.com/gallery/5RkZEL6 Wholesome Post 595 https://imgur.com/gallery/ZFmXEIp Wholesome Post 594 https://imgur.com/gallery/mqFbdyq Wholesome Post 593 https://imgur.com/gallery/VzhDYcl Wholesome Post 592 https://imgur.com/gallery/dlhPf00 Wholesome Post 591 https://imgur.com/gallery/F0iONEA Wholesome Post 590 https://imgur.com/gallery/YduVKmO Wholesome Post 589
I include a Gofundme link in all of my posts to help raise money for people in need. I hope we can help https://www.gofundme.com/f/hfpmv-help-me-catch-up-on-bills
I made a wholesome support server on discord, whether you need support would like to give it or just want to chat with some lovely people and look at some wholesome things it's a nice place. Here's the link if you'd like to join us. https://discord.gg/Y4U422F
I hope this post brightened your day :) and i hope it is a lovely day for you! If you need to talk I am always available on discord (username HaveAGreatDay# 9735) and happy to listen. You matter, and if you are struggling please reach out for help. I made a wholesome support server on discord, whether you need support would like to give it or just want to chat with some lovely people and look at some wholesome things it's a nice place. Here's the link if you'd like to join us. https://discord.gg/Y4U422F

item.title: New low with the "protesters" assaulting Ottawa
item.description: Not me, but feel this needs attention. These assholes harassed staff at the downtown soup kitchen demanding free food and assaulted people when they didn't get their way... let me repeat that so it sinks in: They harassed VOLUNTEERS for free food that's meant for the fucking homeless. Desecrated, and were drinking, dancing, partying on the National war memorial and the "tomb of the unknown soldier" (which is to honor/remember all those who never made it home from war). Also Desecrated the Terry Fox statue, as well as a number of other statues/monuments in the area (Being the capital, Ottawa has a number of statues, memorials, monuments, etc). They had to close most businesses due to harassment, threats, and assaults, as well as the main shopping mall downtown (Rideau Centre), as they were marching and chanting inside without masks and harassing retail workers. Oh, and rocks thrown at windows displaying pride flags. The list goes on of all the issues these fuckwits have caused, people who live & work in the downtown core of Ottawa are being held hostage, and are afraid to leave their residences. And now, someone has actually lost their life. They are walking in the street, holding up vehicles saying "I'm FREE to walk here" Explain to me again how this is a peaceful and respectful protest?
item.dc:creator: ArchonValec

item.dc:creator: dickwitfo

item.title: FUCK CANCER
item.description: Edit: Holy hell, I woke up to so many wonderful and supportive messages. Thank you Imgur, you're truly awesome. We adopted him when he was 8 weeks old as a friend for our 6 month old son Calvin. They truly were the best of buds during their time together. He was the best kitty there ever was. Unfortunately, about 7 months ago, Hobbes was diagnosed with oral squamous cell carcinoma and the cost of treatment was too costly for such a small chance of success. Last night we had no other option but to do what was best for him. We still have 3 more kitties to cuddle with and, in a few hours, my wife will be giving us our 3rd child... so we've got that going for us.
item.dc:creator: AwesomeDad420
